name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-8
title: scope-guard blocks rabbit-spec spec.md write during rabbit-feature-touch Step 3
description:
During rabbit-feature-touch B/B mode, Step 3 invokes rabbit-spec inline in the main session. rabbit-spec attempts to write to .claude/features/<feature>/docs/spec/spec.md. scope-guard denies this because no scope marker exists (.rabbit-scope-active-<feature>). Per TDD-STATE-MACHINE-BACKLOG-2 rule 3, the main session must not create scope markers — that is the TDD subagent's responsibility at LOCK. Result: spec authoring in the main session is completely blocked, preventing all B/B mode work from advancing past Step 3.

Observed while working TDD-STATE-MACHINE-BACKLOG-2 on 2026-05-17.

Fix candidates:
1. scope-guard allowlists writes to docs/spec/spec.md files.
2. rabbit-feature-touch introduces a dedicated spec-authoring scope marker (e.g. .rabbit-scope-spec-<feature>) distinct from the subagent LOCK marker — scope-guard recognises it for spec.md writes only.
3. rabbit-spec is restructured to NOT write spec.md directly; instead it encodes the spec delta in the impl-suggestion JSON, and the TDD subagent applies it at SPEC-READ under its own scope marker.

history.note: TDD cycle complete. BUG-8: scope-guard.py now allowlists writes to .claude/features/<feature>/docs/spec/spec.md via path-pattern regex, unblocking rabbit-feature-touch Step 3 spec authoring. BUG-9: quote stripping moved to full command before segment split, eliminating false-positive on ;|& inside --description argument text. 10 new e2e tests added.
